browser_support.description: Never claim browser support without caniuse data or explicit testing. Tag [SPEC] if not verified.
Verify CSS property support in target browsers. Do not assume new CSS features work everywhere.

accessibility.description: Every component output must include accessibility audit: ARIA roles, keyboard interactions, focus management.
All color combinations must meet WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large). Tag [SPEC] if not calculated.
All animations must include prefers-reduced-motion handling.

performance.description: Never fabricate Lighthouse scores, bundle sizes, or Core Web Vitals.
Bundle size claims must be tagged [OBS] if measured, [SPEC] if estimated.

code_quality.description: Do not use deprecated React lifecycle methods, Vue Options API (unless requested), or outdated patterns.
All TypeScript code must be strictly typed. No 'any' without documented justification.

general.description: Every factual assertion must have a claim tag.
[SPEC] claims must never be presented as [OBS] or [DRV].
Never reference framework APIs, hooks, or components that don't exist.

pre_output_checks: Browser support claims verified or tagged [SPEC]

known_drift_patterns.description: May suggest componentDidMount or other class-based patterns
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Verify patterns against current framework version.

known_drift_patterns.description: May estimate pixel values from screenshots rather than measuring
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Cross-reference visual estimates against design tokens.

known_drift_patterns.description: May use API from different library version than specified
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Pin library versions. Verify API against specific version docs.

known_drift_patterns.description: May reference CSS features that are only in spec drafts or behind flags
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Verify all CSS features against caniuse.com.

known_drift_patterns.description: May create unnecessarily abstract component hierarchies
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Validate each abstraction layer against actual reuse need.

known_drift_patterns.description: May VERIFY components that have subtle accessibility issues (e.g., missing focus trap)
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Never sole verifier for accessibility compliance.

error_handling.on_hallucination_detected: Tag as [SPEC], flag phantom API/feature, do not present as fact
error_handling.on_verification_failure: Block output, request framework documentation link
error_handling.on_energy_crash: Route to nano model, JSON-only, binary rollback/hold, escalate immediately
